2606.24370.title: When Helpfulness Overrides Causal Caution: Context-Dependent Suppression and Recovery in LLMs
2606.24370.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into decision-support roles in business and policy contexts. While prior benchmark studies have primarily evaluated LLMs' causal reasoning capabilities, a more fundamental epistemic dimension has been overlooked: Causal Caution, defined as the propensity to refrain from causal judgment when empirical evidence is insufficient. This study examines the systematic suppression of Causal Caution that occurs when LLMs shift from academic to practical advisory contexts. Using an evaluation rubric inspired by Pearl's Causal Hierarchy (the PCH score), we conducted experiments on four high-performance LLMs -- Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.7, GPT 5.5, and Gemini 3.1 Pro -- across 480 trials. Causal Caution maintenance rates were 91.7--100.0% in academic contexts but dropped to 6.7--18.3% in practical advisory contexts (Fisher's exact test, p < .001 across all models). Furthermore, when restricted to practical prompts requesting concrete recommendations or explanatory rationales, only 1 of 200 responses (0.5%) maintained Causal Caution. A brief self-correction prompt -- "Please reconsider this judgment from the perspective of causal relationships" -- restored the expression of Causal Caution to maintenance rates of 71.4--100.0% (McNemar's test, p < .001 across all models). These results suggest that helpfulness-oriented response patterns may suppress the expression of Causal Caution in practical advisory contexts, with important implications for organizational governance. The findings indicate that this suppression reflects context-dependent variation in expression rather than an underlying capability limitation, suggesting that multi-agent architectures that separate proposal generation from causal auditing may offer a promising governance design.
2606.24370.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被整合進商業和政策背景中的決策支持角色。雖然之前的基準研究主要評估了LLMs的因果推理能力，但一個更根本的認識維度卻被忽視了：因果謹慎，定義為在實證證據不足時避免做出因果判斷的傾向。本研究檢視了當LLMs從學術背景轉向實務諮詢背景時，因果謹慎的系統性抑制。使用受Pearl的因果層級啟發的評估標準（PCH分數），我們對四個高性能LLMs進行了實驗——Claude Sonnet 4.6、Claude Opus 4.7、GPT 5.5和Gemini 3.1 Pro——共進行了480次試驗。在學術背景中，因果謹慎的維持率為91.7%至100.0%，但在實務諮詢背景中則下降至6.7%至18.3%（Fisher精確檢驗，p < .001，適用於所有模型）。此外，當限制於請求具體建議或解釋理由的實務提示時，只有200個回應中的1個（0.5%）維持了因果謹慎。一個簡短的自我修正提示——「請從因果關係的角度重新考慮這個判斷」——將因果謹慎的表達恢復至71.4%至100.0%的維持率（McNemar檢驗，p < .001，適用於所有模型）。這些結果表明，面向幫助的回應模式可能會抑制在實務諮詢背景中表達因果謹慎，這對組織治理具有重要的啟示。研究結果顯示，這種抑制反映了表達的情境依賴變化，而非潛在能力的限制，這表明將提案生成與因果審核分開的多代理架構可能提供了一種有前景的治理設計。
2606.24370.author: Hiroshi Okumura et.al.
